name: Helsinki (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the Finnish national colour and the sea around Helsinki

description: A blue field bearing the city's coat of arms: a golden rowboat on white waves on a blue shield within a black border.
history: Helsinki's coat of arms was granted by Queen Christina of Sweden in 1639 (Helsinki was then part of Sweden); the modern flag, with the arms on a blue field, was adopted in 1947.

features.role: Coat of arms of Helsinki: a golden boat on blue waves
features.symbolism: Helsinki as a maritime city; the boat is a stylised version of the boat from the city's 1639 coat of arms
